text: Debugging is an essential skill in programming. When you notice your code isn't working as expected, the first step is identifying the bug. This means finding the exact line or section of code causing the problem. A systematic approach can save time: reproduce the issue, check recent changes, and test one part at a time. Useful debugging tools include breakpoints in your code editor, which pause execution to inspect values, and built-in error messages. Logging is also a powerful technique—adding print statements or log entries helps you trace what your program is doing step by step. With patience and the right tools, you can solve most bugs efficiently.
